stories.title: Thursday briefing: ​What difference will the ceasefire in the Middle East make, and will it hold?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: The truce offers a reprieve after weeks of turmoil, ​b​ut unresolved disputes and competing interpretations ​of what was agreed, threaten to pull the region back toward crisis​ at a moment’s notice Good morning. On Tuesday, just an hour before the deadline imposed by Donald Trump for Iran to reopen navigation in the strait of Hormuz or face a wave of “civilisation-ending” strikes, a two-week pause in hostilities was announced. After weeks of US and Israeli attacks on Tehra

stories.title: Poll: More Europeans see US as threat than China
stories.summary: The U.S. under Donald Trump is seen as more of a threat than an ally, according to a new POLITICO Pulse survey of six major EU countries. Since returning to power in January 2025, Trump has questioned Washington’s commitment to NATO, threatened to annex Greenland and Canada, hit allies with tariffs and launched a war with Iran that European countries refused to join. Only 12 percent of those polled in March in Poland, Spain, Belgium, France, Germany and Italy saw America as a close ally while 36

stories.title: Spain leads Europe’s shift away from Donald Trump’s US
stories.summary: People in Spain are leading the charge for a stronger, independent Europe amid widespread wariness of Donald Trump’s America, according to a new POLITICO survey. In a poll of people in six EU countries in March, a majority of Spanish respondents — 51 percent — said Washington poses a “threat” to Europe, the largest proportion of all the countries polled. The results also revealed strong Spanish opposition to President Trump’s foreign policies and the U.S. and Israel’s war in Iran. Spain’s respon

stories.title: Orbán’s rival faces uphill battle to unfreeze €17B in EU funds
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Hungarian opposition leader Péter Magyar looks poised to win Sunday’s election and put an end to Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. But even if he does, negotiations to unfreeze €17 billion in EU funding for the country won’t be easy. On the campaign trail, Magyar pledged to quickly free up the EU payouts, which were blocked over Hungary’s democratic backsliding. That would allow him to reset Budapest’s relations with the bloc after years of bad blood betwee

stories.title: What Péter Magyar learned from the last guy to lose to Viktor Orbán
stories.summary: HÓDMEZŐVÁSÁRHELY, Hungary — When opposition leader Péter Márki-Zay failed to topple Prime Minister Viktor Orbán in the country’s 2022 election, many Hungarians said the result showed only an insider could win, using the hardball tactics of the ruling Fidesz party. Péter Magyar, who leads the polls ahead of Sunday’s election , is exactly such a candidate — a Fidesz veteran who was married to Orbán’s former justice minister and understands how to play the prime minister at his own game

stories.title: Euroskeptic think tanks on the rise as Brussels slashes funding for pro-EU groups
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― The European Commission cut millions of euros of funding from established pro-EU think tanks ― just as more Euroskeptic groups have been gaining ground. European think tanks say they’re having to scale back their activities or lay off staff since the Commission scrapped a large chunk of their funding last year; the funding shortfall amounts to €7.8 million, according to French news outlet L’Informé . The funding cut came months after conservative organizations, including Hungary&#8217

And the fact that he has been so successful and he had similar narrative, similar ideologies to US Republicans in terms of immigr

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: JD Vance claims US is not interfering in Hungary election

JD Vance has pushed back against claims that the US is interfering in Hungarian politics, describing the accusations as “darkly ironic”, as a set of polls suggested the opposition Tisza party could win a supermajority in the forthcoming elections. After spending his first day in Budapest excoriating the EU and accusing it o

stories.title: Carnival celebrations at a Hungarian retirement home: János Bődey’s best photograph
stories.summary: ‘I asked them the secret to a happy life. “Good genes,” replied Magdolna, who was 87. “Avoid borrowing money,” added Irén, 86’ When I saw these two elderly ladies dressed as bride and groom in a retirement home in Páty, near Budapest, my first thought was to wonder why they had chosen those particular costumes. I took their picture in the home’s lounge and afterwards we had a long conversation. I asked them about their lives, what they believed was the secret to a long life, and how to preserve

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Trump bejelentette a tűzszünetet, a világ fellélegzett, aztán fél nappal később minden bizonytalan lett
stories.summary: Az olaj ára csökkenni kezdett, a részvénypiacok pedig emelkedtek. Szerda estére azonban komoly ellentmondások látszanak az USA és Irán nyilvános kommunikációjában a tűzszünet feltételeiről és a tárgyalások állásáról. Izrael Libanonban tovább támadja a Hezbollahot. Trump szerint ez nem része a tűzszünetnek, Iránból viszont válaszlépésekkel fenyegettek.

stories.summary: Szerdán Kaposvárra és Nagykanizsára is ellátogatott a Tisza Párt elnöke. Míg Magyar Péter hat, addig Orbán Viktor egy helyszínen, Sopronban kampányolt. Elkötelezett közönség ment ki, de van, aki szerint akkor sem váltja meg az országot, ha lesz pénz.

stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.summary: Orbán offered his support to Putin, likening his assistance to that of a mouse helping a lion in a well-known Hungarian fable, according to a phone conversation transcript obtained by news agency Bloomberg.

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: Médicament : n’attendons pas la présidentielle !
stories.summary: L’urgence d’investir pour la santé des Français Tensions géopolitiques et guerre des prix, perte de vitesse de nos essais cliniques, décrochage industriel, médicaments non remboursés ou non disponibles… Les Français expriment une réelle inquiétude pour l’avenir de leur santé. Ce qu’ils attendent, c’est une France qui les protège, qui les guérit, qui leur permet de vieillir mieux. Pour cela, il est urgent de débloquer l’accès au progrès thérapeutique. stories.title: Ministers unveil ‘right to try’ plan to help disabled people find work
stories.summary: Threat of losing benefits will be lifted but campaigners say more help needed to tackle hostile workplaces The government has unveiled its plan to allow disabled people to try work without fear of losing their benefits, but campaigners warn the policy does not go far enough to tackle hostile workplaces. Legislation laid before parliament on Thursday will mean that people who start work or volunteering no longer automatically face a benefit reassessment, a prospect disabled people said was holdin

stories.title: LA teen loses eye after being shot by US agent at No Kings march, lawyer says
stories.summary: USC student Tucker Collins’s attorney accuses homeland security of ‘overt act of repression’ at Los Angeles protest A freshman at the University of Southern California has lost an eye after he was shot last month with a “less-lethal” projectile by a Department of Homeland Security agent at a No Kings march, according to his attorney. On 28 March, Tucker Collins, 18, took to the streets of downtown Los Angeles to photograph throngs of protesters, who held signs and chanted slogans denouncing the

stories.title: Trump and former loyalist Marjorie Taylor Greene trade jabs as Maga split over Iran widens – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Success or surrender? Iran ceasefire exposes rift in Trump’s Maga movement Sign up for the Breaking News US email Pete Hegseth repeated Donald Trump’s social media comments that Iran will cease uranium enrichment – a condition that Tehran has previously refused to budge on. “Any material they should not have, will be removed right now,” Hegseth said. “The president has been clear from the beginning, there will be no Iranian nuclear weapons.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife found guilty of attempted manslaughter
stories.summary: Prosecutors alleged Gerhardt Konig, 47, had planned to kill Arielle Konig during a birthday trip to Honolulu A Hawaii anesthesiologist who was accused of trying to murder his wife on a cliffside hike last year has been convicted of attempted manslaughter, a lesser charge. A Honolulu jury returned the verdict against Gerhardt Konig, 47, on Wednesday after a day of deliberations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Consumers urged to ‘completely avoid’ UK-caught cod as population plunges
stories.summary: Marine Conservation Society warns that fish numbers have reached dangerous point of decline Consumers should “completely avoid” buying UK-caught cod, the Marine Conservation Society (MCS) has said, as it warned that populations have reached a dangerous point of decline despite zero-catch recommendations. The MCS, an environmental charity, publishes a Good Fish Guide to help consumers and businesses make sustainable seafood choices. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ten years after Brexit, this is the UK: a divided nation frozen in time | Aditya Chakrabortty
stories.summary: Tribalism has not faded over the past decade. Instead, new research reveals our politics has become ever-more polarised and fractious On 23 June 2016 , the British voter changed. Before that day, they picked a party, usually red or blue. By that morning, only two tribes mattered: remain or leave. And they kept mattering long, long after the result was declared. Rather than bin those short-lived and now stale allegiances, voters made them their personas. No longer a “Labour man” or a “Conservativ

stories.title: Abandoned Battle of Britain control tower to become a home for holidaymakers … and six species of bat
stories.summary: Council backs £700,000 plan to save historic landmark at former RAF base in the New Forest A unique RAF watch office that has been crumbling for decades is to be turned into a smart hideaway home to be shared by holidaymakers – and the bats that already use it. The Landmark Trust , which rescues at-risk buildings, has been given permission to convert the ruined property in Hampshire into a holiday retreat with four bedrooms and a roof terrace. Continue reading...

Has

The US and Iran agreed a two-week conditional ceasefire on Tuesday, including a temporary reopening of the strait of Hormuz, after last

stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. Th

stories.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
stories.summary: Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to

The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported t

stories.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag still in custody

stories.title: Family pay tribute to British teenager killed in motorcycle crash in Vietnam

Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventua

stories.title: Greece announces social media ban for under-15s, citing anxiety and sleep problems
stories.summary: PM says ban will come into force in January if it is backed by parliament and calls for united action across EU Greece has announced a social media ban for under-15s from 1 January, with the country’s prime minister, Kyriakos Mitsotakis, citing rising anxiety, sleep problems and the addictive design of online platforms – although he acknowledged it may incur the wrath of some children. “We have decided to go ahead with a difficult but necessary measure: ban access to social media for children un

stories.title: How Pakistan secured ‘biggest diplomatic win in years’ with Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Analysts say Pakistani officials’ efforts led to breakthrough that has helped avert catastrophe, at least for now Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan’s leaders had almost lost hope. After more than two weeks of frantic negotiations, phonecalls and diplomatic summits to try to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, it looked like the conflict might instead be escalating into Islamabad’s worst nightmare . In a cabinet meeting held at about 5pm on Tuesday, Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Shari

stories.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
stories.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

stories.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?

stories.title: How Pakistan brokered a US-Iran ceasefire, and what's next
stories.summary: The US, Israel and Iran have agreed to cease hostilities for two weeks after Islamabad mediated a temporary truce. The deal is a major achievement for Pakistan, but what comes next is likely to be even more complicated.

stories.summary: The presidency says it is taking action as it fears fuel shortages could lead to public disorder.

stories.title: Man fined after breaking into viral hippo Moo Deng's enclosure

stories.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
stories.summary: It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.

stories.title: Syrian accused of wartime torture for al-Assad denies charges in Netherlands court
stories.summary: The trial is based on universal jurisdiction, a legal principle that allows suspects to be prosecuted for international offenses such as war crimes even if they are committed in another country.

stories.title: Lebanon 'separate skirmish' and not part of Iran ceasefire deal, Trump tells US media
stories.summary: Israel has rarely struck central Beirut since the outbreak of the latest Israel-Hezbollah war on 2 March but has regularly struck southern and eastern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs.

stories.title: Nigeria begins mass trial of 500 terrorism suspects
stories.summary: Very few people are ever prosecuted following terror attacks with suspects often held without trial.

stories.title: Digital detox: Offline club events in Netherlands offer screen-free social connection
stories.summary: Participants in Amsterdam and Utrecht join “offline club” events, handing in their phones to spend time on creative activities and in-person interaction.

stories.title: Multiple airstrikes hit Beirut, emergency crews respond to fires

stories.title: Watch: Fire damages roof of Rio's Olympic velodrome

In light of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many came to regard his longstanding ties to Russia and personal friendship with Vladimir Putin as self-serving. stories.title: Anti-war protesters rally outside US embassy office in Tel Aviv

stories.summary: Pakistani police destroyed hectares of opium poppy fields in a tribal region near the Afghan border, as farmers said poverty leaves them few alternatives.

stories.title: Iranians form human chains on bridges amid tensions with US and Israel
stories.summary: Hundreds of people in Iran formed human chains on bridges after a government call to show support and protect infrastructure during heightened tensions.

stories.title: Five teens to go on trial in Sweden over attempted murder of Iranian dissident
stories.summary: Sweden’s security service Sapo says Iran has ramped up its targeting of dissidents since the start of the Iran war, accusing them of conscripting organised crime groups to carry out “acts of violence” on Swedish soil.

stories.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
stories.summary: Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime. Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at

stories.title: Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas
stories.summary: Politicians warn party’s pledge to ‘punish’ countries seeking justice for slavery will harm and isolate Britain Commonwealth politicians say they will not back down from seeking reparations as UK public figures, including a former Reform insider, warn the rightwing party’s pledge to “punish” countries seeking justice for slavery would harm and isolate Britain. This week, Reform UK said they would halt visas for nationals of countries formally demanding reparations from Britain if they took power

stories.title: Explainer: What is in Iran’s 10-point ceasefire plan and will the US agree to it?
stories.summary: Two-week ceasefire comes after Trump spoke to Pakistan’s leaders, with China also believed to be exerting influence over Tehran Middle East crisis – live updates US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire with Tehran saying it will reopen strait of Hormuz The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Tuesday barely an hour before Donald Trump’s deadline to obliterate Iran was set to expire, with Tehran agreeing to temporarily reopen the strait of Hormuz. Israel also agreed to the ceasefire,

stories.title: Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn
stories.summary: Category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds at centre in excess of 150km/h Tropical Cyclone Vaianu forming in the Pacific could bring life-threatening winds and heavy rain to New Zealand later this week, forecasters have said, with strong wind watches issued for the entire North Island. The category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds around the centre in excess of 150km/h, MetService said on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe cannot bet on a post-Trump US turning back to sanity | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: If the president’s first term didn’t inoculate the American body politic against tyranny, there is no guarantee that a second dose will work Donald Trump is a despot and the US is a democracy. These things can be true simultaneously but not indefinitely. There is now deadlock in the struggle between a president who would be king and a constitution drafted in repudiation of monarchy. But it is a battle to the death. Tyranny will either break the spirit of the republic or be quelled by it. Since t

stories.title: A veszélyeztetett gyerekek többsége láthatatlan a rendszernek
stories.summary: Egy hónapos munkáját tette közzé a Falakon Túl Munkacsoport, ami a zárt intézetekben bántalmazott gyerekek jövőjéért küzd. A szakemberek konkrét javaslatokat is megfogalmaztak. Szerintük sokszor még a dolgozók is úgy látják: nincs értelme jelezni a bántalmazást, úgysem történik semmi.

stories.title: Esélytelen, hogy az olajárak visszatérjenek a háború előtti szintre
stories.summary: Egyes elemzők szerint még ahhoz is nagy szerencse kell, hogy pár héten belül ne induljon újra a háború Irán, Izrael és az Egyesült Államok között.

stories.title: Hétnapos munkahetek, visszatartott fizetések, túlóra nincs – kínai vendégmunkásokkal készült jelentés a szegedi BYD-építkezés körülményeiről
stories.summary: Egy amerikai civil szervezet ötven vendégmunkással beszélgetett, a teljes jelentés májusban jelenik meg.

stories.title: Caramel a legújabb videójában Nagy Ervinnel közösen buzdít szavazásra
stories.summary: Bár a videó a zenész Instagram-oldalán jelent meg, de a Tisza arculatával zárult.

stories.title: Hann Endre reálisnak tartana egy 74 százalékos választási részvételt, ami rekord lenne
stories.summary: A Medián szerint a Tisza olyan stabil kétharmadot szerezhet április 12-én, amilyenre 2014 óta nem volt példa.

stories.title: Nem indul a választáson Z. Kárpát Dániel
stories.summary: A jobbikos politikus 16 év után távozik a parlamentből, de a közéletet nem hagyja el.

stories.title: A Fidesz idén is a Bálnában várja a választás eredményét
stories.summary: A békepárti kormánypárt elfoglalja vasárnap este a honvédelmi központot.

stories.title: A Tisza szerint Hoppál Péter étellel vásárol szavazatokat, Hoppál szerint ez hazugság, ő ételt kínált egy választónak
stories.summary: Videó készült arról, ahogy Hoppál azt mondja egy választónak, a nevére hivatkozva enni kap valahol, ami a Tisza pécsi jelöltje szerint választási csalás, Hoppál szerint segítség.

stories.title: Hogyan tanultuk meg a kihalás szót? – három történet a pusztításról
stories.summary: Turpeinen könyve újra és újra ugyanarra a kérdésre tér vissza: mit jelent az, hogy egy faj végleg eltűnik és vajon a felismerés, hogy mit tettünk, elegendő-e ahhoz, hogy másképp viselkedjünk a világgal, amelyben élünk.

stories.title: J. D. Vance szombaton Pakisztánban tárgyal az iráni békéről
stories.summary: Donald Trump elképzelése szerint az Egyesült Államok bevételre tenne szert a Hormuzi-szoroson áthaladó hajók után.

stories.title: 15 év börtönre ítélték a dílert, aki eladta Matthew Perrynek a halálát okozó ketamint
stories.summary: Perry négy nappal a halála előtt 25 fiola ketamint vásárolt attól a nőtől, aki az ügy legsúlyosabb ítéletét kapta.

stories.title: Tíz éve még hemzsegtek a békák Farmoson, idén kevesebb mint százat mentettek meg
stories.summary: A Tápió Natúrpark gyászolja a helyi békapopulációt.

stories.title: Bajnokok Ligája negyeddöntő: PSG–Liverpool 2-0
stories.summary: A PSG óriási fölényben játszott az első félidőben, rengeteget volt náluk a labda és Doué gólt is szerzett. A másodikra feljavult kicsit a Liverpool, de mégis a PSG lőtt még egy gólt, és nagyon magabiztosan győzött.

stories.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
stories.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

stories.title: How to insulate your household from soaring energy bills
stories.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, record-breaking heat waves and blackouts, producing electricity at home looks more appealing than ever.

stories.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

stories.title: Three gunmen 'neutralised' in shooting incident near Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: Authorities said a comprehensive investigation is being carried out with law enforcement units under the coordination of the chief public prosecutor's office.

stories.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: No Israeli diplomats are currently in Turkey and the Istanbul consulate has been empty for the past two-and-a-half years.

stories.title: Trump's Iran ultimatum: When does it expire and how serious is his threat to 'blow up everything'?
stories.summary: US president warns a civilisation could be about to disappear if the regime in Tehran does not respond to his ultimatum after six weeks of war by Tuesday night. What are the possible scenarios? Euronews explains:

stories.title: Has Artemis II shown we can land on the Moon again?
stories.summary: The Artemis II mission has been near flawless to date, but has the test flight shown Nasa is ready to send humans to the lunar surface?

stories.title: Over 180 dead or missing in Mediterranean in last 10 days, UN migration agency says
stories.summary: The IOM has previously highlighted the Central Mediterranean as the deadliest migration corridor in the world.

stories.title: Greek parliament recommends lifting MP immunity in EU farm subsidy fraud scandal
stories.summary: Nine of the 11 MPs submitted written statements to the committee, while former Rural Development Minister Kostas Tsiaras and MP Notis Mitarakis appeared in person to make their case.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran war could now 'spiral out of control'
stories.summary: In a series of direct warnings and as the US deadline for Iran approached later on Tuesday, Qatar’s foreign ministry called for “a way of ending the war before it spirals out of control”, adding that there was still time for diplomacy to prevail, “before it escalates beyond anyone’s control”.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released by Iraqi militant group, official says
stories.summary: Like many freelancers, Shelly Kittleson often worked on a shoestring budget and without the protections afforded by large news organisations to staff.

stories.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
stories.summary: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

stories.title: Wireless Festival cancelled after Kanye West blocked from coming to UK
stories.summary: West was due to headline the festival in July but drew criticism over past antisemitic comments.

stories.title: Russia and China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening Hormuz strait as US deadline for deal looms
stories.summary: One-fifth of the world's oil typically passes through the strait and Iran's chokehold during the war has sent energy prices soaring around the world.

stories.title: Construction workers block roads in France over soaring fuel prices
stories.summary: Construction workers set up a roadblock on the Nantes ring road to protest rising fuel costs, saying the sharp increase is putting pressure on their businesses.

stories.title: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.
